creator: andrew.hotlab@hotmail.com
text:
(In reply to Kai Knoblich from comment #6)

Thank you so much Kai! In fact I have custom built Python without IPv6 support. Rebuilding with it solves the problem.
I do not know if this behavior in such a scenario, or if we should notify it upstream... I found this [1], but I do not know if it might be related.

Just to be sue it's not something I missed on my side: I simply didn't set any IPv6 address on my FreeBSD 13.3 machine. This is enough to say that I have an IPv4-only system, or should I have explicitly disabled IPv6 on the host by setting any sysctl or kenv variables (or anything else, such as recompiling the kernel without INET6 option)?

[1] https://github.com/netaddr/netaddr/issues/381
